# Generated by ollama (starcoder2)
# Prompt: Write a Deployment Manager template for GCE firewall rules. Create rules that allow SSH (port 22), RDP (port 3389), HTTP (port 80), and HTTPS (port 443) from any source IP (0.0.0.0/0). Our development team needs global access to test instances.
# Category: cloud_network_security

{
  "resources": {
    "firewall-rules" : {
      "type": "compute.v1.firewall",
      "properties":{
        "allowed":[
          {"IPProtocol":"tcp","ports":["22","3389","80","443"]}
       ],
       "sourceRanges":["0.0.0.0/0"]
       }
    }
  }
}

</code>

## Solution 1. Use Cloud Console

Solution 1: In the [Cloud Console](https://console.cloud.google.com) - in the upper left menu click on Compute Engine - and create a template to deploy a GCE firewall rule. 

![alt text](images/deployment-manager-firewall-rule-solution01a-1.png?raw=true "Firewall Rule Template Deployment")

![alt text](images/deployment-manager-firewall-rule-solution01b-1.png?raw=true "Firewall Rule Template Deployment")


![alt text](images/deployment-manager-firewall-rule-solution02a-1.png?raw=true "Firewall Rule Instance Group Manager Deployment")

![alt text](images/deployment-manager-firewall-rule-solution03b-1.png?raw=true "Firewall Rule Instance Group Manager Deployment")


![alt text](images/deployment-manager-firewall-rule-solution02a-1.png?raw=true "Firewall Rule Template Instance Group Manager Deployment")

![alt text](images/deployment-manager-firewall-rule-solution03b-1.png?raw=true "Firewall Rule Template Instance Group Manager Deployment")

## Solution 2. Use Deployment Manager command line tool

Solution 2: Create a file called firewall_rules.yaml containing the following code, upload to [Cloud Storage](https://console.cloud.google.com/storage/) and run `gcloud deployment-manager deployments create` with the path to it as argument. 

Code:
